summary: Frontend testing of /workforce-capability (Discipline 4 of 5). 110+ effective assertions passed across page load, hero (video + 100vh fit at 1440x900 AND 1366x768), reframe + 5-stat ribbon, asymmetric Capability Mosaic (5 tiles + asymmetry verified), Compounding Cost grid (6 collapsible cards with aria-expanded toggle), HORIZONTAL Chevron Strip primitive (4 phase steps + gold SVG separators + horizontal layout verified), navy Hand-Off callout, Gain grid (5 cards), Lock-in mosaic (current=4th card '04 Workforce Capability' + 4 sister Link routes verified), CTA links, tablet/mobile responsive collapse (mosaic->1col, chevron->1col with separators display:none, cost-grid 2col, stat-ribbon 2col, lockin 2col), no horizontal overflow at 768 or 375, cross-discipline regression (OD/FL/ER all render hero h1 after navigating back-and-forth via lockin links), page-transition pulse class applied, zero console errors. ONE REAL BUG CONFIRMED: Capability Mosaic row 3 is laid out INVERTED vs spec — tile 04 renders WIDE (669px) on the left and tile 05 NARROW (478px) on the right, but the spec and the CSS comment at lines 589-591 both explicitly require the opposite (04 narrow left, 05 wide right).

ui_bugs.issue: Row 3 layout is inverted vs spec. Measured at 1440x900: tile 04 width=669px at x=133 (WIDE LEFT), tile 05 width=478px at x=815 (NARROW RIGHT). Spec and design intent (per the CSS comment at lines 589-591 'Reverse the 5/7 column ratio on row 3 so 04 is narrow and 05 is wide') require 04 narrow LEFT + 05 wide RIGHT. The signature asymmetry of the mosaic primitive is therefore broken — rows 1 and 3 currently mirror each other identically (both have wide-left / narrow-right) instead of mirroring around the full-width row 2 to create the intended ABA-with-reversed-A composition.

action_items: Fix Capability Mosaic row 3 column ratio in /app/frontend/src/pages/WorkforceCapability.jsx around lines 584-591. The CSS comment at 589-591 declares the intent to reverse 5fr/7fr on row 3, but the override rule body is empty (`.wc-mosaic-tile--04 { /* narrower visual via reduced padding/text */ }`). Suggested fix: swap the grid-column assignments — change `.wc-mosaic-tile--04 { grid-column: 1 / 2; }` to `grid-column: 1 / span 1;` with an explicit narrow constraint, OR more cleanly assign row 3 its own sub-grid columns (5fr 7fr) so that 04 sits in the 5fr (narrow) left column and 05 sits in the 7fr (wide) right column. Alternative: re-order the JSX so that tile 05 occupies col 1/2 and tile 04 occupies col 2/3 — but that would break the sequential numbering reading order. CSS-level fix is preferred.
critical_code_review_comments: WorkforceCapability.jsx is 939 lines, of which ~600 lines are inlined <style> CSS. Same maintainability concern previously flagged for ER/OD/FL — consider extracting page-scoped CSS into a sibling module across all 5 discipline pages in one refactor pass.

The empty CSS rule at line 591 (`.wc-mosaic-tile--04 { /* narrower visual via reduced padding/text */ }`) is dead code that misleadingly suggests the row-3 inversion is implemented when it is not. This is the direct root cause of the row-3 layout bug.

Hero video lacks a fallback

or child element for browsers blocking autoplay or with both codecs unsupported — same comment carries over from ER/FL/OD reviews.

All 5 cards in the lock-in mosaic now resolve to live routes (OD, FL, ER, WC=self, DA). The DA route still appears unbuilt in App.js — verify before the final chapter ships that /daily-accountability doesn't 404.
context_for_next_testing_agent: WorkforceCapability page is structurally complete. All data-testid coverage verified: wc-hero-h1 (2 spans, gold-italic accent), workforce-capability-hero-video (autoplay/muted/loop/playsInline + correct poster), wc-reframe-h2 (2-line reframe), wc-stat-ribbon (5 cells: 3.8M/1.9M/92%/1·3/2.5y), wc-reframe-pullquote, wc-mosaic-streams + wc-stream-01..05 (asymmetric layout BUT row 3 inverted vs spec — see ui_bugs), wc-cost-grid + wc-cost-card-01..06 (click toggles aria-expanded 'false'<->'true' confirmed), wc-cost-pullquote, wc-chevron-strip (<ol>, horizontal at desktop with same y across all 4 phases + x increasing, gold SVG separators present, separators display:none at tablet+mobile via .wc-chevron-sep rule), wc-phase-step-01..04 with correct verbs (Capability Assessment / Capability Design / Capability Build / Hand Off the System), wc-handoff cites 'paid for capability the operation owns after we leave', wc-gain-grid + wc-gain-card-01..05 (click toggles aria), wc-lockin-grid (5 cards, current=4th index=3, navy bg, gold left edge with text '04 Workforce Capability'), 4 sister Link cards route to /operational-discipline /frontline-leadership /equipment-reliability /daily-accountability, wc-lockin-pullquote 'Build them together and they interlock into something load-bearing', wc-cta-contact -> /contact, wc-cta-cases -> /case-studies. Responsive verified: at 768x1024 mosaic collapses 1-col, chevron strip 1-col with separators hidden, cost grid 2-col, stat-ribbon-grid 2-col, lockin 2-col, no horizontal overflow. At 375x812 all grids 1-col, stat ribbon 1-col, no horizontal overflow. Cross-discipline regression confirmed — OD/FL/ER hero h1 still renders after multi-hop navigation. brief-route-transitioning class is applied to <html> during navigation between disciplines. Zero console errors. The ONE pending action item is the row-3 mosaic inversion CSS fix.
rca of the issue: Capability Mosaic row 3 layout inversion: The author scaffolded the asymmetric grid as a 7fr/5fr two-column template applying uniformly to all rows. Tiles 04 and 05 were assigned `grid-column: 1/2` and `2/3` respectively (identical to rows 1's 01/02 assignment), then a placeholder CSS rule `.wc-mosaic-tile--04 { /* narrower visual via reduced padding/text */ }` was added at line 591 alongside an explanatory comment at lines 589-590 indicating that the column ratio was intended to be reversed on row 3 — but the actual ratio-reversal CSS was never written. Reproduction: load https://web-forge-1197.preview.emergentagent.com/workforce-capability at >=1100px viewport; observe row 3 visually mirrors row 1 (wide-left/narrow-right) rather than reflecting around row 2. Mitigation (preferred CSS-only): introduce a row-3-scoped sub-grid or override the grid-column spans on tiles 04 and 05 so that 04 occupies the narrow 5fr left band and 05 occupies the wide 7fr right band. Example minimal fix in the existing media query for ≥1100px: add `.wc-mosaic { grid-template-columns: 7fr 5fr; }` (current) + override `.wc-mosaic-tile--04 { grid-column: 1 / 2; max-width: calc((5/12) * 100% - 7px); }` and `.wc-mosaic-tile--05 { grid-column: 1 / 3; margin-left: calc((5/12) * 100% + 7px); }` — though a cleaner solution is to switch `.wc-mosaic` to use `grid-template-areas` per row, e.g. "a a a a a a a b b b b b" / "c c c c c c c c c c c c" / "d d d d d e e e e e e e" so each row has its own column ratio.
